prompt: Construct a ReclaimOps Offline Operations Suite supporting an in-store recyclable textiles buyback program with integrated receiving/QC, traceability, and service workflow. Staff access the English UI through an HTMX-driven browser experience optimized for fast, form-based work on a local network: a Front Desk Agent opens a buyback ticket, captures clothing category, condition grade, and estimated weight in pounds, then the screen shows an instant estimated payout and any promotional tier (for example, “+10% bonus on 20–49 lb, +15% on 50+ lb”) with a hard cap such as $200.00 per ticket and $3.00/lb maximum. At receiving, a QC Inspector records actual weight and inspection outcomes; if the final payout differs by more than 5% or $5.00 (whichever is higher), the interface forces variance confirmation and then requires a second user (Shift Supervisor) to re-enter their password to approve the adjustment before the ticket can be completed. The same UI supports store and table/private-room management for customer service intake areas: hosts can open, merge, and transfer tables/rooms with visible state changes (available, occupied, pre-checkout, cleared) and an on-screen activity timeline for accountability. A Notification Center provides offline customer communication by logging in-order messages and one-tap dialing from the workstation (no SMS/email); staff can apply templates such as accepted, rescheduled, arrived, completed, or refunded, choose whether a customer prefers calls only, and record delivery attempts with retry reminders for failed contact. Administrators manage club organizations and member lifecycle (departments/groups, join/leave/transfer, and bulk import/export from CSV) to support staff rosters and partner clubs, while Operations Managers view core reports (order volume, load factor, revenue, refund rate) by date range and route/store and can export data with supervisor approval. The backend uses Flask to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by HTMX partial updates, with SQLite as the local system of record for tickets, pricing rules, QC results, batch genealogy, table states, member history, and immutable audit logs. The pricing engine evaluates rule-based tiers, caps, and eligibility windows (MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time), stores both estimated and actual calculations, and enforces dual-control approval for sensitive actions including price overrides, refunds, and schedule adjustments; every such action writes a tamper-evident log entry with before/after values and operator identity. The Quality & Traceability System applies sampling rules (for example, inspect 10% of lots with a minimum of 3 items per lot, escalating to 100% if two nonconformances are found in a day), supports quarantine with disposition outcomes (return to customer within 7 days, scrap, or concession acceptance with supervisor sign-off), and maintains batch-level traceability from procurement to receiving to issuance to finished goods, including recall-list generation by batch and date. Security and privacy are enforced entirely offline: TLS is used on the local network with locally managed certificates, passwords are stored with salted hashing, sensitive fields (phone numbers, addresses) are masked by default and encrypted at rest using an application-managed key stored outside the SQLite file, and session tokens use signed, time-limited cookies with anti-replay nonces plus CSRF protection and input validation to mitigate XSS/injection. File uploads (for imports/exports) are restricted by allowlisted formats (CSV only), size-limited (5 MB), and validated via file type checks and cryptographic hashing; exports can require supervisor approval and optionally apply a visible watermark and per-user attribution to discourage unauthorized redistribution.
